figures.caption: Fig. 3. Qualitative examples of group-conv (top) vs. edge-MPC (bottom). Green trajectories show the robot trajectories. Red dots are pedestrians. Black enclosures are the group representations. Blue dots are the goals.

Fig. 4. Left: The robot used in the real world experiment. Middle: The crossing scenario. Yellow trajectories illustrate edge-MPC’s behavior. Blue trajectories illustrate crowdattn-RL’s behavior. edge-MPC tried to walk behind the groups while crowdattn-RL often cut in front. Right: The flow scenario. edge-MPC performed a wide maneuver to avoid all the groups while crowdattn-RL followed the group in front and cut through the two incoming groups.
